A Footprint employee via the dashboard

linked_attestations.description: A list of assoicated attested devices

AuthOrgMember.description:
Member of an organization
 Info on the currently authed user

phone_numbers.description: Info on the phone numbers provided

allOf.description: Information about the provided TIN
Info on the website provided
Details about the business

names.description:
Information about names (DBA/Legal) that were submitted and found associated with the
 business

people.description: People associated with the business (submitted or found)

allOf.description: Watchlist results from screening the submitted + found people + biz names

fields.description:
List of data identifiers to decrypt. For example, `id.first_name`, `id.ssn4`,
 `custom.bank_account`

reason.description:
Reason for the data decryption. This will be logged.
 The reason must be provided either here or in the client token

transforms.description:
A list of filter and transform functions to apply to each decrypted datum.
 Omit or leave empty to apply no transforms
 Can find more information on allowed transform functions on our docs

CollectedDataOption.description:
Represent the options of allowed CollectedData.
 Some CollectedData variants only have a single allowable CollectedDataOption.
 Other CollectedData variants may have multiple Options of assortments of data that can be
 collected. Each CollectedDataOption maps to a list of IdentityDataKinds that are represented by
 the CDO

allOf.description: None if the doc is unassigned.

is_live.description: The target is_live for the copied playbook

allOf.description: When a link was requested to be sent, the method by which the link was sent

send_to_bo_ids.description:
The list of business owner IDs to which we should send KYC links via both email + SMS.
 Will generate and return links for all BOs regardless.

allow_reonboard.description:
Allow the same user to onboard onto this playbook multiple times. It is generally not
 recommended to enable this setting. When this is enabled, you lose:
 - Protection against one user incurring many charges at your tenant
 - Ability for in-progress onboardings to continue where they left off
 When false, onboarding will no-op for repeat onboardings.

deprecated_can_access_data.description:
When provided, used to populate the deprecated `can_access_data` field. When not provided,
 can_access_data is computed from must_collect_data and optional_data.

access_reason.description: Access reason to use during proxy decryptions

allOf.description:
A certificate and key to authenticate via mTLS. Omit to skip client-certificate
 authentication

method.description: HTTP method: POST, GET, PUT, PATCH, DELETE

name.description: a friendly name for this proxy config

pinned_server_certificates.description:
A list of PEM-encoded x509 certificates or chains that are either CAs or self-signed. These
 certificates will be used to verify the root-of-trust of the certificate presented by the
 proxy

url.description: The proxy destination URL. Can include path and query params

tag.description: Any string to tag the user

omit_email_invite.description:
A feature only used by employees to allow inviting users to a tenant without sending them an
 email

expires_at.description: The time at which the token (and link) expire

link.description:
A Footprint link embedding the `token` that can be sent to this user to allow them to
 complete the flow. This is useful to send in an automated message to the end user.

token.description:
A short-lived token that can be passed into the Verify SDK to allow the user to complete the
 flow. This is useful when you'd like to open a native interface inside your app for the user
 to complete the flow.

CursorPaginatedAuditEvent.description: Describes an event relevant to security as seen by tenants.

next.description: The `cursor` parameter to provide in order to request the next page of results.

name.description: The human-readable name of the document to display to the user

attributes.description: The list of CDOs that had a DI that was edited

is_prefill.description: True when the data added in this timeline event was added via a one-click onboarding

delete_all.description: When true, deletes all data in the vault.

fields.description:
List of data identifiers to delete. For example, `id.first_name`, `id.ssn4`,
 `custom.account_id`

allOf.description: Non-null for images uploaded via bifrost
Non-null for images uploaded via bifrost

is_extra_compressed.description:
When true, we detected that the user had a slow internet connection and the client
 compressed the image more than normal to allow for a faster upload.

data.description: Metadata on the data that exists in this vault.

allOf.description: The kind of entity: Person or Business

transforms.description: Decrypted transforms of this attribute, if already decrypted

value.description: Decrypted, plaintext value if already decrypted

FilterFunction.description: Represents a data transform to apply to underlying plaintext behind a data identifier. Specify only one of the following fields. See more here: https://docs.onefootprint.com/articles/vault/proxy#filter-functions

expires_at.description: The time at which this token will expire.

vault_fields.description: The list of fields that are allowed to be vaulted by this token

IdDocKind.description:
This is the set of document kinds that are verifiable with incode. They are govt-issued docs,
 and they all support having a selfie uploaded alongside them.

allOf.description: Ingress content type

kind.description:
dba, legal, etc. not an enum bc we don't need to have it be, and we also don't have full
 info from middesk docs

sources.description:
what places a vendor found information about this name.
 Does not include watchlist information

verified.description: It's possible we get a name submitted / found but did not try to verify, hence Option

submitted.description: People can be submitted (BOs) or found via filing records, or other sources

verified.description: Whether or not the TIN was verified in the IRS TIN database

business.description: Watchlist entries for the submitted + found biz names

people.description: Watchlist entries for the submitted + found people

fields.description:
List of data identifiers to decrypt. For example, `id.first_name`, `id.ssn4`,
 `custom.bank_account`

signing_key.description: A hex-encoded key for computing `hmac-sha256` signatures

IntegrityResponse.description: A key-value map with the corresponding hex-encoded hash values

Iso3166TwoDigitCountryCode.description:
list of valid iso3166-alpha-2 country codes, from https://datahub.io/core/country-codes#data
 eventually we should maybe just pony up and pay for the subscription to iso: https://www.iso.org/publication/PUB500001.html
 Channel islands does not have a country code

show_all.description: When true, shows hidden incomplete vaults that didn't complete a signup challenge

Limited information about member of organization

 Intended to be non-sensitive information only, that may be shared between tenants/partner

TimelineOnboardingDecision.description: Describes the outcome of an onboarding decision that took place on the user.

ran_rules_in_sandbox.description:
When true, the rules were ran for this decision despite being in sandbox mode - we should
 show the rules outcome drawer

TimelinePlaybook.description: ObConfiguration serialization used inside of an OnboardingDecision

note.description: Optional note with more context on what we're asking the user to do

clear_support_email.description: Used to set support_email to null

clear_support_phone.description: Used to set support_phone to null

clear_support_website.description: Used to set support_website to null

fields.description:
List of data identifiers to decrypt. For example, `id.first_name`, `id.ssn4`,
 `custom.bank_account`

reason.description: Reason for the data decryption. This will be logged

transforms.description:
A list of filter and transform functions to apply to each decrypted datum.
 Omit or leave empty to apply no transforms.
 Can find more information on allowed transform functions on our docs

version_at.description:
When provided, decrypts the user's data as it existed at the provided timestamp.
 Provided as an ISO 8601 timestamp string, like `2024-01-01T12:00:00Z`.

UserDecryptResponse.description: A key-value map with the corresponding decrypted values

UserDeleteResponse.description: A key-value map of identifier to whether the identifier was successfully deleted in the vault

wrapped_record_keys.description: Maps record paths to their wrapped record keys.

 This allows editing data, re-verifies data, and then re-triggers decision engine

reuse_existing_bo_kyc.description:
When true, reuses existing BOs' KYC results on the same playbook.
 When false, requires the existing BOs to re-complete KYC.
 Can only be true for KYB playbooks

kind.description:
Allow onboarding onto the specific playbook.
 This allows editing data, re-verifies data, and then re-triggers decision engine
